title: Bhavangad Fort
tagline: Bhavangad Fort — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: Bhavani Gad Fort (Marathi: भवानगड किल्ला, Hindi: भवानी गड क़िला, transliteration: Bhavа̄ngad Killa) is a fort located at Saphale - Madhukar Nagar 3 km from village Kelve, in Palgha
overview.description: Bhavani Gad Fort (Marathi: भवानगड किल्ला, Hindi: भवानी गड क़िला, transliteration: Bhavа̄ngad Killa) is a fort located at Saphale - Madhukar Nagar 3 km from village Kelve, in Palghar district, of Maharashtra. This fort is in very dilapidated condition. The outer walls, steps, parapets, bastions etc. were built without using limestone at many places.

nearestAirport.name: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Aagarwadi
topPlaces.description: Agarwadi (Aagarwadi) is a small village in Maharashtra, India, having a population around 3600. The village is located at a distance of 6 km (3.7 mi) from Saphale Railway station, west side.

topPlaces.name: Kelwa
topPlaces.description: Kelwa or Kelve is a town in Maharashtra, India. It is served by the Kelve Road railway station on the Western line of the Mumbai Suburban Railway which extends from Churchgate to Dahanu Road.

topPlaces.name: Kelwa Beach
topPlaces.description: Kelwa Beach, also known as Kelva or Kelwe Beach, is a beach in Maharashtra, India. It is a popular weekend getaway for tourists from Mumbai.

topPlaces.name: Makunsar
topPlaces.description: Makunsar is a village in Maharashtra, India, located 6 kilometres (3.7 mi) away from Saphale's railway station (Western railway), on the Saphale-Palghar road. Gaon is well known for Temples, because more numbers of temples are seen in this gaon.

topPlaces.name: Kelve Fort
topPlaces.description: Kelve Fort, or Kelva Fort (Marathi: केळवे किल्ला), is a fort in the Thane district of Maharashtra, located 12.5 km from Palghar. This fort is amidst the Casuarina plantation on the Kelwa Beach.

topPlaces.name: Saphale
topPlaces.description: Saphale (also spelled Safāle or Saphala) is a town in Palghar Taluka in Palghar District in Maharashtra, India, north of the mouth of the Vaitarna River. According to Census 2011 information, the location code or village code of Saphale village is 552293.

topPlaces.name: Dativare fort
topPlaces.description: Dativare Fort / Hira Dongar is a fort located 72 km from Vasai, in Palghar district, of Maharashtra, India. The fort is located at a commanding point upon a small hillock beside the Vaitarna River, which runs until Arnala Fort.

topPlaces.name: Datiware
topPlaces.description: Datiware is a village in the state of Maharashtra (Palghar taluk, Palghar district) in India on the northern bank of Datiware creek at the mouth of the river Vaitarna. This area is especially famous for its birds and is a popular destination for birdwatchers in winter.

items.note: Agarwadi (Aagarwadi) is a small village in Maharashtra, India, having a population around 3600. The village is located at a distance of 6 km (3.7 mi) from Saphale Railway station, west side.
Kelwa or Kelve is a town in Maharashtra, India. It is served by the Kelve Road railway station on the Western line of the Mumbai Suburban Railway which extends from Churchgate to Dahanu Road.
Kelwa Beach, also known as Kelva or Kelwe Beach, is a beach in Maharashtra, India. It is a popular weekend getaway for tourists from Mumbai.

Makunsar is a village in Maharashtra, India, located 6 kilometres (3.7 mi) away from Saphale's railway station (Western railway), on the Saphale-Palghar road. Gaon is well known for Temples, because more numbers of temples are seen in this gaon.
Kelve Fort, or Kelva Fort (Marathi: केळवे किल्ला), is a fort in the Thane district of Maharashtra, located 12.5 km from Palghar. This fort is amidst the Casuarina plantation on the Kelwa Beach.

Saphale (also spelled Safāle or Saphala) is a town in Palghar Taluka in Palghar District in Maharashtra, India, north of the mouth of the Vaitarna River. According to Census 2011 information, the location code or village code of Saphale village is 552293.

Dativare Fort / Hira Dongar is a fort located 72 km from Vasai, in Palghar district, of Maharashtra, India. The fort is located at a commanding point upon a small hillock beside the Vaitarna River, which runs until Arnala Fort.

Datiware is a village in the state of Maharashtra (Palghar taluk, Palghar district) in India on the northern bank of Datiware creek at the mouth of the river Vaitarna. This area is especially famous for its birds and is a popular destination for birdwatchers in winter.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bhavangad Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bhavangad Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bhavangad Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai (56 km) and the nearest railway station is Kalyan Junction (Mumbai) (56 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bhavangad Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bhavangad Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bhavangad Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Bhavangad Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bhavangad Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Aagarwadi, Kelwa, Kelwa Beach, Makunsar, Kelve Fort.
